req.title: Run payroll — variant 3
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-074 specifies a payroll-run endpoint with five distinct concerns: (1) happy-path full computation and record creation, (2) webhook emission, (3) 409 conflict when a run is already in progress, (4) atomic rollback on internal failure, and (5) boundary of exactly one active employee. I selected EP for the normal success path (valid hr-admin role, active employees), NEG for the 409 conflict and 500 rollback paths, STC to verify the state machine transition idle→running→completed, DT to cross the decision of concurrent-run guard, and BVA for the single-employee boundary. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that reset state before execution and uses deterministic employee/payroll IDs seeded per test.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_happy_path_creates_stubs_and_webhook
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin session token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
At least two active employees exist, seeded by <seed_active_employees> fixture
No payroll run is currently in progress
Webhook receiver mock is active via <webhook_mock> fixture
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with valid period payload using hr-admin token
Poll or await response for completed status
Assert webhook_mock received the payroll.completed event
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with body containing state=completed
Each active employee has exactly one pay-stub with gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit log contains one entry for the run
Webhook mock captured exactly one event with type=payroll.completed and matching run_id
trace: R-WEB-074: emit webhook event for external consumers
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_happy_path_creates_stubs_and_webhook(
    hr_admin_token, seed_active_employees, webhook_mock, api_client, cleanup_payroll_runs
):
    employees = seed_active_employees(count=2)
    payload = {"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"}

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    run_id = body["id"]
    assert body["state"] == "completed"

    stubs_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == len(employees)
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]

    audit_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/audit-log?resource=payroll-run&id={run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["entries"]) >= 1

    events = webhook_mock.received_events()
    payroll_events = [e for e in events if e["type"] == "payroll.completed"]
    assert len(payroll_events) == 1
    assert payroll_events[0]["data"]["run_id"] == run_id

tests.name: test_payroll_run_409_when_run_already_in_progress
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin session token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
A payroll run for the same period is already in state=running, created by <seed_in_progress_run> fixture
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with the same period while an in-progress run exists
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating concurrent run conflict
No new payroll-run record is created
trace: R-WEB-074: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_409_when_run_already_in_progress(
    hr_admin_token, seed_in_progress_run, api_client
):
    in_progress_id = seed_in_progress_run(period="2024-06")
    assert in_progress_id is not None

    payload = {"period": "2024-06", "type": "monthly"}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"]["code"] in ("CONCURRENT_RUN", "RUN_IN_PROGRESS")

    runs_resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/payroll-runs?period=2024-06",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert runs_resp.status_code == 200
    runs = runs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(runs) == 1
    assert runs[0]["id"] == in_progress_id

tests.name: test_payroll_run_500_rolls_back_atomically
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin session token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
Fault injection configured via <inject_payroll_fault> fixture to simulate mid-run DB error
actions: Enable fault injection that causes a DB error after partial stub writes
Query pay-stubs and payroll-run state after the 500 response
expected: No pay-stub records exist for the failed run (full rollback)
No payroll-run record persists in completed or partial state
Audit log does not contain a completed entry for this run
trace: R-WEB-074: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-074: a failed run shall not partially commit

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transitions_idle_to_completed
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin session token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
One active employee seeded via <seed_active_employees> fixture
No payroll run exists for the target period
Endpoint supports state query while run is in flight via <slow_payroll_mode> fixture
actions: Enable slow_payroll_mode to pause the run mid-processing
GET /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} after completion — capture final state
expected: Initial state after POST is running or accepted (not completed)
Final state after resume is completed
State sequence follows idle -> running -> completed with no skipped states
trace: R-WEB-074: triggers payroll run (state machine coverage)
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_state_transitions_idle_to_completed(
    hr_admin_token, seed_active_employees, slow_payroll_mode,
    resume_payroll_run, api_client, cleanup_payroll_runs
):
    seed_active_employees(count=1)
    slow_payroll_mode.enable()

    payload = {"period": "2024-08", "type": "monthly"}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 202)
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    mid_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert mid_resp.status_code == 200
    mid_state = mid_resp.json()["state"]
    assert mid_state in ("running", "accepted", "pending")

    slow_payroll_mode.disable()
    resume_payroll_run(run_id)

    final_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert final_resp.status_code == 200
    assert final_resp.json()["state"] == "completed"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_boundary_single_active_employee
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin session token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
Exactly one active employee exists, seeded by <seed_active_employees> fixture
No payroll run is currently in progress for the target period
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs for a period with exactly one active employee
expected: Exactly one pay-stub record is created
Pay-stub has gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Webhook emits exactly one payroll.completed event
trace: R-WEB-074: pay-stub records created (boundary: minimum active employee count = 1)
R-WEB-074: BVA lower boundary on active employee set
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_boundary_single_active_employee(
    hr_admin_token, seed_active_employees, webhook_mock, api_client, cleanup_payroll_runs
):
    employees = seed_active_employees(count=1)
    assert len(employees) == 1

    payload = {"period": "2024-09", "type": "monthly"}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    run_id = body["id"]
    assert body["state"] == "completed"

    stubs_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == 1
    stub = stubs[0]
    assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
    assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
    assert stub["net_pay"] == stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]

    events = webhook_mock.received_events()
    matching = [e for e in events if e["type"] == "payroll.completed" and e["data"]["run_id"] == run_id]
    assert len(matching) == 1
